breakdown: This PR refactors rewards box numeric extraction by creating a centralized `_get_raw()` helper that searches multiple fallback keys with robust handling for zero candidates, NaN, inf, and empty strings, then pipes results through a defensive converter. The test file provides both internal unit tests covering edge cases and an E2E hallucination provocation test. Quality observations: Good architectural separation with extracted helper, excellent test coverage across permutations, and explanatory comments on non-obvious logic decisions. Minor deduction for `Any` typing in internal helpers and lack of typed error classes.
key_changes: Extract `_get_raw()` helper function with fallback search logic handling edge cases (NaN, inf, zero candidates)
Replace multiple `coerce_int()` calls with centralized `_DNC.convert_value()` using fallback key lists
Add `rewards_box_robustness.py` test file with internal unit tests and E2E hallucination provocation
Improve `next_level_xp` handling to default to 0 when absent instead of game-state defaults
Add explanatory WHY comments for non-standard boolean/numeric handling in LLM output
